_status_note: verified_in_runner = CANCEL BOOKING section presence confirmed in source. The full cancel path (STATE restore + _parseEscalationType + Firestore update) requires live API testing to verify end-to-end.
title: Cancel booking: AI refuses direct cancellation after booking confirmed
failing_behavior: After booking was confirmed via _submitDirectBooking, when customer said 'cancel my appointment', the AI responded 'I'm not able to cancel appointments directly. Please call the salon.' The booking was never cancelled in Firestore.
root_cause: Three missing pieces: (1) _submitDirectBooking cleared biz._bookingState and didn't restore name/phone — Claude had empty STATE. (2) No CANCEL BOOKING section in system prompt. (3) _parseEscalationType didn't include 'cancel'. All three had to be present simultaneously for cancel to work.

conversation.content: I'd like to cancel my appointment

precondition: biz._bookingState = {services:['Manicure'], staff:'Helen', name:'Jane Smith', phone:'4085551234', lang:'en'}. biz._lastBookingId = 'DLC-TEST99'. This state is set by _submitDirectBooking after a prior booking.
notes: The cancel flow requires all three pieces simultaneously. Breaking any one disables cancel. Invariants documented in tests/memory/booking_cancel_modify_flow.md.
